purpose: Owns the entire branch management surface inside Git Manager. Renders three internal subsystems: (1) a current-branch header with ahead/behind badges plus 'New' and 'Move' action buttons, (2) a custom SVG BranchGraph (commit topology with colored lanes, merge curves, ref labels, and HEAD highlighting) computed via buildGraphLayout from up to 80 commits returned by api.gitGraph, and (3) collapsible Local/Remote branch lists with inline search filtering, ahead/behind chips per upstream-tracking branch, and per-branch action buttons (switch/checkout, merge, delete). Owns several local dialog implementations (ConfirmDialog and MergePreviewDialog) plus a multi-step 'Move uncommitted changes to a new branch' flow that orchestrates stash → createBranch → stashPop with rollback semantics. Branch operations call gitCheckout, gitCreateBranch, gitDeleteBranch, gitMerge, gitStash/StashPop, gitAheadBehind, gitGraph, gitLog (for merge preview commits) and dispatch GIT_SET_BRANCHES / GIT_SET_CURRENT_BRANCH / GIT_SET_STATUS to refresh global state.
gotchas: classifyBranches uses a naive heuristic — any branch name containing '/' is classified as remote, so any local branch with a slash (e.g. feature/foo) will be misclassified. Consult b.upstream / b.remote fields if available rather than relying on the slash check.
Delete branch tries safe `-d` first; on failure it shows a force-delete dialog with the original error in errorDetail — do not bypass this two-step flow.

Switching branches with uncommitted changes opens a switch dialog that offers stash-first; if checkout fails after stashing, performSwitchBranch must call gitStashPop to roll back, otherwise the user's work is lost in the stash list.

performMoveChanges executes stash → createBranch → stashPop. Each step must rollback on failure; if pop fails after a successful create, the changes remain in the stash list and the user is told so explicitly.
BranchGraph fetches up to 80 commits via api.gitGraph; it depends on commits including .parents (multi-parent for merges) and .refs arrays — backend must populate these fields.
Branch name validation rejects whitespace, ~, ^, :, ?, *, [, \, leading -, trailing ., or consecutive '..' — keep this in sync with git's stricter ref-name rules if extending.
aheadBehindMap is fetched per local branch with an upstream — this is N sequential awaits inside a useEffect; for repos with many upstream-tracking branches this can be slow.
patterns: Local + remote sections use independent collapse state (localCollapsed default open, remoteCollapsed default closed).
Inline-defined ConfirmDialog and MergePreviewDialog (NOT shared with SafetyDialogs.jsx) — closes on Escape via window keydown listener.
Merge action loads a preview by running `gitLog(repoPath, 50, [`${currentBranch}..${sourceBranch}`])` to display the commits that would be merged.
Graph lane colors derive from getComputedStyle (--color-in-progress) at module load, then use a fixed palette of 10 stable colors keyed by lane index.
Operation errors show in a banner above the lists and auto-clear after 8 seconds.
GraphCell uses cubic-Bezier curves to draw merge connections with 0.4 vertical control offsets — keeps merge curves visually smooth across rows.
conventions: All git API calls go through window.electronAPI.git* and check `result.success` before reading `result.data`. Errors surface via setOperationError state.
Refresh helpers `refreshBranches` (branches+currentBranch) and `refreshAll` (also status) are reused by every mutation handler.
Inline SVG icons (IconBranch, IconChevron, IconPlus, IconTrash, IconMerge, IconSwitch, IconMove, IconWarning, IconCheck, IconCloud, IconSearch, IconStar, IconRemote) — no external icon library.
